name: Digit value confusion
description: Learner reads the digit itself instead of its place value.

name: Place name confusion
description: Learner confuses adjacent place names like tens and hundreds.
common_wrong_patterns: digit 4 in 3456 -> tens instead of hundreds

name: Reads digit not value
description: Learner names the face value rather than the place value.

name: Digit count confusion
description: Learner compares number length or digit count incorrectly.

name: Left-to-right comparison error
description: Learner compares the wrong place first when ordering numbers.

name: Reverses order language
description: Learner reverses ascending and descending order directions.
common_wrong_patterns: ascending order written largest to smallest

name: Drops zero place
description: Learner ignores a zero placeholder in expanded notation.

name: Adds digits not values
description: Learner adds or lists digits instead of place values.

name: Wrong place expansion
description: Learner expands a number into incorrect place values.

name: Place alignment error
description: Learner mixes ones, tens, and hundreds across columns.

name: Ones tens mixup
description: Learner combines ones and tens inconsistently.

name: Ignore carry
description: Learner fails to regroup in addition.

name: Place value confusion
description: Learner carries or combines values into the wrong place.

name: Digit reversal
description: Learner reverses the digits of the computed answer.

name: Digit add without regroup
description: Learner adds each digit pair without carrying to the next place.

name: Subtract smaller from larger digit
description: Learner subtracts digits by size instead of by place and direction.

name: Borrow not applied
description: Learner recognizes the need to borrow but does not adjust the next place correctly.

name: Forgets reduce tens
description: Learner borrows to the ones place but forgets to reduce the tens.

name: Counts additively only
description: Learner treats multiplication as repeated addition without scaling correctly.

name: Factor order confusion
description: Learner confuses the factors and the total in multiplication.

name: Repeated addition error
description: Learner counts rows or columns without combining all groups correctly.
common_wrong_patterns: array counted by one dimension only

name: Division as subtraction only
description: Learner applies subtraction once rather than equal sharing or grouping.

name: Inverse fact confusion
description: Learner multiplies instead of dividing or swaps dividend and divisor roles.

name: Group count confusion
description: Learner confuses number of groups with size of each group.

name: Swap numerator denominator
description: Learner reverses the numerator and denominator of a fraction.

name: Counts unshaded parts
description: Learner uses the unshaded parts instead of the shaded parts.

name: Denominator as shaded
description: Learner thinks the denominator tells how many parts to shade.

name: Rounds down instead of nearest
description: Learner chooses the lower multiple even when the number should round up.

name: Rounds up instead of nearest
description: Learner chooses the higher multiple even when the number should round down.

name: Rounds to wrong place
description: Learner rounds to an adjacent place value instead of the named place.

name: Product as factor confusion
description: Learner uses the product itself as the missing factor instead of finding the unknown factor.

name: Additive factor error
description: Learner adds known values or uses additive reasoning instead of multiplicative reasoning.

name: Off by one factor
description: Learner chooses a nearby factor instead of the exact missing factor.

name: Ignore remainder
description: Learner gives only the quotient and leaves out the remainder.

name: Use divisor as remainder
description: Learner gives the divisor as the remainder instead of a value smaller than the divisor.

name: Round up quotient remainder
description: Learner increases the quotient to avoid leaving a remainder.

name: Scale one part only
description: Learner changes only the denominator or only the numerator when making an equivalent fraction.

name: Add same number to both terms
description: Learner adds the same number to numerator and denominator instead of multiplying.

name: Copy numerator denominator
description: Learner repeats the original fraction instead of generating an equivalent one.

name: Compares denominator only
description: Learner decides which fraction is larger by looking only at the denominator, even when numerators differ.

name: Larger denominator means larger fraction
description: Learner thinks the fraction with the larger denominator must be greater when numerators are the same.

name: Reverses greater less symbol
description: Learner understands the fractions but chooses the comparison symbol in the opposite direction.

name: Reduces numerator only
description: Learner divides only the numerator when simplifying a fraction.

name: Reduces denominator only
description: Learner divides only the denominator when simplifying a fraction.

name: Keeps unsimplified fraction
description: Learner recognizes an equivalent fraction but leaves it in the original unsimplified form.

name: Finds one part only
description: Learner divides the set by the denominator but forgets to multiply by the numerator.

name: Uses denominator as answer
description: Learner gives the denominator as the number of objects instead of finding the fraction of the set.

name: Uses whole set instead of fraction part
description: Learner repeats the whole set size instead of calculating the required fraction of it.

name: Adds denominators too
description: Learner adds the denominators as well as the numerators when adding like fractions.

name: Keeps one numerator only
description: Learner keeps one addend numerator instead of combining both numerators.

name: Adds across fraction digits
description: Learner mixes numerator and denominator operations and forms an unrelated fraction.

name: Subtracts denominators too
description: Learner subtracts the denominators as well as the numerators when subtracting like fractions.

name: Uses subtrahend numerator
description: Learner uses only the second numerator as the answer numerator instead of subtracting the numerators.

name: Keeps starting numerator
description: Learner leaves the first numerator unchanged instead of subtracting the second numerator.

name: Assumes all fractions less than one
description: Learner thinks every fraction must be less than one whole.

name: Equal numerator denominator confusion
description: Learner does not recognize that equal numerator and denominator make exactly one whole.

name: Reverses fraction whole comparison
description: Learner reverses less than and greater than when comparing a fraction to one whole.

name: Whole number only from improper fraction
description: Learner writes only the whole number and ignores the leftover fractional part when converting an improper fraction to a mixed number.

name: Uses numerator as remainder
description: Learner keeps the original numerator as the mixed-number remainder instead of using what is left after making wholes.

name: Adds whole and numerator without scaling
description: Learner adds the whole number and numerator when converting to an improper fraction instead of multiplying the whole by the denominator first.
Learner counts a single row or column of an array and treats it as the total.
name: Array row count only

description: Learner confuses the number of groups with the number in each group when dividing.
name: Group size confusion

common_wrong_patterns: How many groups of 3 in 12 -> 12 divided by answer choice backwards
description: Learner reverses the grouping relationship and applies division in the wrong direction.
name: Reverse division

description: Learner extends a pattern using the wrong operation, usually adding instead of multiplying or vice versa.
name: Additive vs multiplicative confusion

description: Learner identifies the wrong flow-diagram rule from the input-output relationship.
name: Incorrect rule

description: Learner chooses the wrong operation when completing or checking a number sentence.
name: Operation confusion

common_wrong_patterns: calls a rectangle a square because both have 4 sides
description: Learner identifies a shape by an incomplete or incorrect property set.
name: Shape property confusion

common_wrong_patterns: cube identified by edge count alone
description: Learner confuses faces, edges, and vertices when identifying 3D objects.
name: Faces edges confusion

common_wrong_patterns: vertical mirror line marked on a non-symmetric half
description: Learner places or identifies a line of symmetry incorrectly.
name: Mirror line confusion

description: Learner uses or compares the wrong unit for a measurement situation.
name: Unit confusion

description: Learner swaps the roles of the hour hand and minute hand when reading time.
name: Hour minute confusion

common_wrong_patterns: perimeter of 4 by 3 rectangle given as 12
description: Learner multiplies side lengths when the task requires adding them, or adds when multiplication is needed.
name: Add vs multiply confusion

description: Learner counts only the outer boundary units instead of the full area in square units.
name: Count boundary only

common_wrong_patterns: bar at 6 read as 3 on a scale of twos
description: Learner reads bar-graph or data scale intervals incorrectly.
name: Scale misread
